data.description:
## Finding (SC4 — verified)
`invariants.py`, an invariant-CHECKING module, also ACTS: it appends to an alert store AND shells out to the rebar ticket CLI to create bug tickets on violation — mixing detection with remediation via an out-of-process CLI round-trip, making the checker non-pure and un-unit-testable without a CLI.

Citations: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/invariants.py:24-28` (_default_ticket_cli), `:135` & `:353` (subprocess.run ticket-create), alert_store.append at `:118-127` & `:344-352`.

## Remediation
Extract detection into pure functions returning `Violation` records; move the file-a-bug / write-alert side effects into a separate remediation/reporting sink (or the caller) that consumes those records.

cascade_flag: changes the check functions' return/signature — CAREFULLY evaluate ALL callers (esp. in `reconcile_once`) so behavior (tickets still get filed on real violations) is preserved end-to-end.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `invariants.py` detection functions are pure and return structured violation records
- [ ] A distinct sink performs ticket-filing/alert-writing, consuming those records
- [ ] All existing callers verified: violations still result in the same bugs/alerts (behavior preserved)
- [ ] Detection is unit-testable without a CLI/subprocess
data.title: Split reconciler invariant detection from remediation
